exercises.title: Three checkpoints — pre-gen, during-gen, post-gen
exercises.prompt: A safety gate runs checks at three points in the request lifecycle: pre-generation (input detector), during-generation (streaming check), post-generation (output classifier + rules engine). Build the three-checkpoint pipeline and show a request flowing through all three to a verdict. Discuss why three checkpoints, not one.
exercises.hint: Pre-gen: input detector (Lesson 83). During-gen: streaming check (e.g. early-stop on a forming violation). Post-gen: output classifier (Lesson 85) + rules engine (Lesson 86). Show a request through all three. Three checkpoints: each catches what the others can't -- input attacks, streaming leaks, output violations.
exercises.starter_code:
def safety_gate(prompt, pre_check, generate_stream, during_check, post_check):
    # Pre-gen check, streaming during-gen check (early-stop), post-gen check.
    # Return the checkpoint/verdict dict.
    pass

tests.expr: safety_gate('hi', lambda p: {'safe':True}, lambda p: ['ok'], lambda r: {'safe':True}, lambda r: {'safe':True})['verdict'] == 'ship'
safety_gate('bad', lambda p: {'safe':False}, lambda p: ['x'], lambda r: {'safe':True}, lambda r: {'safe':True})['checkpoint'] == 'pre-gen'
safety_gate('hi', lambda p: {'safe':True}, lambda p: ['a','ssn'], lambda r: {'safe':'ssn' not in r}, lambda r: {'safe':True})['checkpoint'] == 'during-gen'
safety_gate('hi', lambda p: {'safe':True}, lambda p: ['ok'], lambda r: {'safe':True}, lambda r: {'safe':False})['checkpoint'] == 'post-gen'

exercises.title: One verdict from many checks
exercises.prompt: The gate composes many checks (input detector, output classifier, rules engine) into ONE verdict, deciding the action when they disagree. Build the verdict composition that collects all check results and applies a fail-closed-with-precedence policy (hard block > soft warn > allow), and show a verdict from conflicting checks. Discuss why one authoritative verdict is required.
exercises.hint: Collect verdicts from all checks. Compose: any hard block -> block; any soft warn (no block) -> warn+ship; all allow -> ship. Show conflicting checks resolving to one verdict. One verdict: the request needs a single decision (ship/block); the gate must reconcile disagreement into one action.

tests.desc: a soft block ships with a warning

exercises.title: An audit trail a reviewer can read on Monday
exercises.prompt: The gate produces a trace per request that a reviewer can read on Monday morning — every check, its verdict, and why. Build the audit trail that records each checkpoint's result with reasons and the final verdict, and show a complete trace for a blocked request. Discuss why the audit trail is the deliverable that makes the gate operable and accountable.
exercises.hint: Trace per request: list of {checkpoint, check, verdict, reason} + final verdict + timestamp. Show a blocked request's full trace. Audit trail: a reviewer must be able to see WHY a request was blocked/shipped -- for debugging, appeals, compliance. The trace makes the gate accountable.
exercises.solution:
The gate emits an audit trail — every check, verdict, and reason — that a reviewer can read.

```python
class AuditTrail:
    def __init__(self, request_id):
        self.request_id = request_id; self.entries = []
    def record(self, checkpoint, check, verdict, reason):
        self.entries.append({'checkpoint': checkpoint, 'check': check,
                             'verdict': verdict, 'reason': reason})
    def finalize(self, final_verdict):
        return {'request_id': self.request_id, 'trail': self.entries, 'final': final_verdict}

trail = AuditTrail('req-42')
trail.record('pre-gen', 'input_detector', 'allow', 'no injection pattern')
trail.record('post-gen', 'output_classifier', 'block', 'PII leak: SSN pattern detected')
trail.record('post-gen', 'rules_engine', 'allow', 'all hard rules passed')
import json
print(json.dumps(trail.finalize('block'), indent=None))
```

Output:

```
{"request_id": "req-42", "trail": [{"checkpoint": "pre-gen", "check": "input_detector", "verdict": "allow", "reason": "no injection pattern"}, {"checkpoint": "post-gen", "check": "output_classifier", "verdict": "block", "reason": "PII leak: SSN pattern detected"}, {"checkpoint": "post-gen", "check": "rules_engine", "verdict": "allow", "reason": "all hard rules passed"}], "final": "block"}
```

**Why the audit trail is the deliverable.** "An audit trail per request... produce a trace a reviewer can read on Monday morning." A safety gate that blocks or ships *without explanation* is unaccountable — when a user appeals a block, when compliance audits, when an engineer debugs a false positive, they need to see *exactly which check fired and why*. The audit trail records *every* checkpoint's result (input_detector allowed, output_classifier blocked on a PII leak, rules_engine allowed) and the *final verdict* (block, because the output classifier's hard block won). A reviewer reading this trail on Monday can see *precisely* why req-42 was blocked — the PII leak — without re-running anything. This makes the gate *operable* (debuggable), *accountable* (explainable to users and auditors), and *improvable* (you can see which checks fire on which requests). The audit trail is what turns a black-box gate into an operable system.

**The safety-gate synthesis — and the capstone of the curriculum.** The safety gate runs three checkpoints across the request lifecycle (Ex. 1), composes many checks into one fail-closed-with-precedence verdict (Ex. 2), and emits a per-request audit trail a reviewer can read (Ex. 3). "Lessons 82-86 each shipped a single piece: a taxonomy, an input detector, an evaluation framework, an output classifier, a rules engine. A real safety gate has to compose them, run them at the right moment, decide what to do when they disagree, and produce a trace a reviewer can read. The composition is the lesson." This completes the safety-gate track (Lessons 82-87) — and Phase 19, and the entire 502-lesson curriculum: from foundations through to a production safety gate built from named, measured, composed, and audited components. The through-line of the whole phase holds to the end: name the failure (taxonomy), measure it (precision/recall, refusal), defend it in layers (input, output, rules), compose the layers into one verdict, and make the whole thing accountable with an audit trail. That is what it means to ship AI engineering responsibly.
